title: erigeron alpinus var. pyrenaicus
intro: Erigeron alpinus var. pyrenaicus, also known as Pyrenean daisy, is a variety of the alpine daisy plant, native to the Pyrenees mountain range in Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erigeron alpinus var. pyrenaicus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or oil, and it can also be used topically as a salve or cream to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
